text.title: Data-Driven Requirements Engineering Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the data-driven-requirements methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (frame business question): Translate vague ask into measurable business question.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the frame business question gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (capture baseline): Pull baseline metric snapshot with provenance.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the capture baseline gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (set target): Define measurable target with direction + by_when.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the set target gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (design instrumentation): Events, properties, dashboard ties; review with analytics-eng.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the design instrumentation gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (post launch measure): Compare against target; trigger learn/iterate/kill decision.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the post launch measure gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for data-driven-requirements do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
